description: Fix race conditions, partial-revert bugs, resource leaks, and missing error feedback in the Chrome extension side panel UI.

userStories.title: Fix npm search race condition causing stale results to overwrite newer ones
userStories.description: As a user searching for plugins, I want search results to always reflect my latest query so that I don't see stale results from a slower earlier request.
acceptanceCriteria: handleSearchChange tracks the latest query and discards responses from older queries
If user types 'sla' then quickly types 'slack', a slow response for 'sla' arriving after 'slack' does not overwrite the 'slack' results
npmSearchTimer ref is cleared in the useEffect cleanup to prevent timer firing after side panel closes
userStories.notes:
Two issues in `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx`:

**Race condition (lines 68-88):** `handleSearchChange` debounces 400ms then calls `searchPlugins(query)`. The `.then()` at line 80 calls `setNpmResults(result.results)` unconditionally. If a slow response for an earlier query arrives after a faster response for a later query, it overwrites the correct results. Fix: use a monotonically increasing counter ref. Increment on each search dispatch, capture the value in the closure, and only call `setNpmResults` if the counter still matches.

**Missing cleanup (line 48, lines 142-268):** `npmSearchTimer` is a ref holding a debounce timeout ID. The `useEffect` cleanup at line 267 removes the message listener but never calls `clearTimeout(npmSearchTimer.current)`. Add `clearTimeout(npmSearchTimer.current)` to the cleanup function. The timer firing after unmount would call `searchPlugins()` → `sendRequest()` → `chrome.runtime.sendMessage()` against a potentially dead context, causing a console error.

userStories.title: Fix BrowserToolsCard toggle-all partial revert on mixed success/failure
userStories.description: As a user toggling all browser tools, I want the UI to correctly reflect which tools were actually toggled so that the UI does not desync from the server.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard.handleToggleAll sends a single batch request to the server instead of N individual requests
If the batch request fails, all tools revert correctly (same behavior as PluginCard which uses setAllToolsEnabled)
userStories.notes:
In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx` at lines 51-63, `handleToggleAll` sends N individual `setBrowserToolEnabled` requests via `Promise.all`. Compare with `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx` lines 57-69 which correctly uses `setAllToolsEnabled` (a single server request).

The problem: if 3 of 5 individual requests succeed and 2 fail, `Promise.all` rejects and the catch handler reverts ALL tools to their pre-toggle state. But the server has already toggled the 3 that succeeded — creating a desync between UI and server state.

Fix: Check if `bridge.ts` already has a `setAllBrowserToolsEnabled` function or similar batch endpoint. If not, add one that calls `sendRequest('config.setAllBrowserToolsEnabled', { enabled })`. Then use it in `handleToggleAll` like `PluginCard` does. If the server does not support a batch endpoint for browser tools, the MCP server side may also need a handler — check `platform/mcp-server/src/` for the config.setAllToolsEnabled handler and add an analogous config.setAllBrowserToolsEnabled.

Alternatively, if adding a new server endpoint is too complex for one story, the simpler fix is: on `Promise.all` rejection, re-fetch the server state via `fetchConfigState()` to get the actual state of each tool, instead of blindly reverting to `preToggleRef.current`.

userStories.title: Add user feedback on plugin update and remove failures
userStories.description: As a user managing plugins, I want to see an error message when a plugin update or removal fails so that I know the action did not succeed.
acceptanceCriteria: When removePlugin() rejects, an error alert or toast is shown to the user indicating the failure
When updatePlugin() rejects, an error alert or toast is shown to the user indicating the failure
The error is displayed temporarily (3-5 seconds) and auto-dismisses, consistent with existing error patterns (ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS)
userStories.notes:
In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx`:

**handleUpdate (lines 136-139):** `updatePlugin(pluginName).catch(() => {})` silently swallows all errors. The user clicks 'Update', nothing happens, no feedback.

**handleRemove (lines 117-134):** The `.catch()` at line 127 clears `removingPlugins` but gives no user feedback. The plugin remains in the list (correct) but the user doesn't know why removal failed.

Follow the existing error pattern used for install errors: `App.tsx` already has `installErrors` state (a `Map<string, string>`) at line 44 and passes it to `SearchResults` → `NpmPluginCard`. Create analogous `updateErrors` and `removeErrors` state maps (or a combined `pluginErrors` map keyed by `pluginName`). Pass the error to `PluginCard` (via `PluginList`) and display it using the same `Alert` pattern already used in `PluginCard.tsx` lines 160-163 for toggle errors.

For `handleUpdate`, the error message should come from the rejection reason: `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)`. For `handleRemove`, same pattern.

The `ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS` constant from `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/constants.ts` can be reused for auto-dismissal, or the errors can persist until the next successful operation (consistent with `installErrors` which persist until the install succeeds or a new search starts).
